title: Crumbly Smoky Brazilian Veal Carpaccio
description: Thinly sliced veal kissed with smoky paprika and lime, topped with toasted coconut flakes for a crisp, adventurous bite.
instructions: Slice the veal tenderloin into 1‑2 mm thick slices using a cold meat slicer or a sharp knife.
Arrange the veal slices on a chilled plate in a single layer.
Whisk together lime juice, lime zest, olive oil, smoked paprika, crushed pimenta malagueta, salt, and black pepper in a small bowl.
Drizzle the seasoned sauce evenly over the veal, ensuring even coverage.
Let the veal rest for 10 minutes at room temperature to allow the flavors to meld.
Sprinkle the toasted coconut flakes over the top for a crumbly, smoky crust.
Broil the plate in a preheated broiler for 30‑45 seconds per side, watching closely to char the edges without cooking the inside.
Remove from the broiler, garnish with chopped parsley, and serve immediately while the veal remains raw inside.
